source.title: The benefits of errorless learning for serial reaction time performance in Alzheimer's disease.

confidence_rationale: Direct AD-side test of MECH-172's prediction. AD patients can in fact acquire a perceptual-motor sequence under errorless conditions, demonstrating that the procedural-learning channel is functional even when episodic memory has substantially failed. Mapping fidelity is good; transfer risk is low. Source quality is moderate -- specialty journal, modest sample. The errorless qualification is a real wrinkle: it is not unconditional preservation, it is preservation when error monitoring is offloaded.
failure_signatures: If MECH-172 were wrong and AD's pathology degrades both systems together, AD patients would fail the SRT in errorless conditions too. They did not.

Errorless > errorful in AD specifically (not just generally) suggests an interaction between feedback monitoring and procedural acquisition that the simple architectural-separation reading does not predict.

mapping.source_claim_statement: AD patients can acquire a 6-element perceptual-motor sequence under errorless conditions but not under errorful (standard SRT) conditions. The errorless advantage is larger in AD than in healthy older adults.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-172's AD half: the model-free habit/procedural channel is preserved in AD when not contaminated by failed error monitoring (which is the consolidation-pipeline component AD breaks). Schmitz shows the channel works when the part of the pipeline AD breaks is bypassed -- consistent with the architectural-separation reading.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Schmitz finds preservation specifically under errorless conditions, not unconditionally. MECH-172 in its current statement claims procedural memory is 'preserved'; the more careful reading is that the procedural acquisition channel is preserved but the integration with feedback monitoring is not. This is a refinement to MECH-172, not a refutation -- and arguably a strengthening, since it predicts which conditions produce visible preservation and which do not.
mapping.source_context: AD patients (early-to-middle-stage; specific MMSE range in paper) and matched healthy older adults; standard SRT task with errorless preliminary phase manipulation.

confidence_components.notes: JAD specialty journal; modest sample; result is real but the errorless caveat refines rather than confirms the simple version of MECH-172.
